title: Calceolaria coquimbensis
taxonomy: Calceolaria coquimbensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Calceolariaceae. It belongs to the genus Calceolaria, which consists of about 200 species of flowering plants. The plant is native to Chile. As a member of the family Calceolariaceae, it is closely related to other plants such as Penstemon and Keckiella. The classification of Calceolaria coquimbensis is still a subject of ongoing research in the field of botany.
uses: Calceolaria coquimbensis is a species of flowering plant native to Chile. There is limited information available about the medicinal uses of Calceolaria coquimbensis. However, some species of the Calceolaria genus are known to have potential medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
